text:
There was a boy named Toby who lived alone in the vast woods. Every day, he would wake up to the chorus of birds and tread through the dew-kissed grass. Toby knew the woods like the back of his hand, knowing every hidden glade and ancient oak.

One sunny afternoon, he came across an old, wooden hut. Its front door creaked when Toby pushed it open. Inside was a curious sight—a old, raggedy woman with wild hair, sitting by a crackling fireplace. She looked up and smiled.

"I've never seen anyone like you before," she said. "You must be Toby."

"I am," Toby replied. "And you live here alone?"

"It's true," she confirmed. "Never have any visitors."

Toby settled into a long seat. "How'd you end up here?"

She pondered. "I lost everything. My family. My home. I became lost. Then I found this hut. I've lived here ever
